notes: v2.5.7 - Sourcing & Stock: Inventory and Purchasing are now one module with a single dashboard, sharing one set of numbers. Vendors get a full page with an approval lifecycle, encrypted credentials, trustworthy scoring and supplier corrective actions (SCARs). Purchase orders now enforce approval authority, show four clear status badges (ordered, received, settled, approval), and handle returns/adjustments and dock receiving as first-class records; a partial PO no longer strands the remaining lines. Requisitions use one composer with a history tab and split shortages per traveler. Inventory's movement ledger, cycle counting, certificate/quarantine holds and a consumables view all work, and undoing a consumption now genuinely marks it undone. Quality NCRs, dispositions, repairs and scrap are real audited actions - scrap works again after three faults, including an entry point that had never worked. Several wrong numbers are corrected: shortage analysis now counts units (it had under-ordered by roughly 488,000 units), the Shortages tile can report a real shortage, around 10,000 records now name the right creator, and catalog search finally returns results. Refusals now explain themselves and the app no longer states totals it cannot know. You will also be told when your desktop app is out of date. Early pilot (authorised engineers only): view a shared CATIA session from inside AOS via Open remotely / Connect, with a loading indicator, an application-state readout, a focus mode and window tabs; connection handling is steadier with more reliable first-attempt connects and fewer mid-session freezes. A new Fleet Monitoring view under Security and Monitoring shows machine and service health and running versions across the AOS server and the CATIA workstation.
